steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence makes a main claim (processor power has drastically increased) and then elaborates with a surprising consequence introduced by "even" — even once-revolutionary dual-core technology is now commonplace. The punctuation must correctly attach that elaboration to the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma joining two independent clauses: Creates a comma splice with no coordinating conjunction. ✗
  • Choice B — malformed punctuation: The punctuation pattern is grammatically invalid. ✗
  • Choice C — dash + 'that': A dash correctly introduces the elaborating clause; "that" marks it as subordinate, making the whole construction grammatical. ✓
  • Choice D — period: Splits the elaboration into a fragment — "That the once-revolutionary..." has no predicate. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
